required_skills: Aerospace systems engineering and system-level design
Structural analysis and finite element analysis (FEA)
Flight mechanics and vehicle performance analysis
Requirements management and system verification and validation (V&V)
Thermal analysis and heat transfer modeling
Material science and aerospace-grade material selection
Technical documentation and engineering drawing interpretation
Design for manufacturability and design for reliability (DFR)
preferred_skills: Experience with space launch vehicles or satellite platforms
Knowledge of DO-178C or DO-254 avionics certification standards
Familiarity with AS9100 quality management systems
Experience with hypersonic or supersonic vehicle design
Familiarity with NASA or ESA mission design processes
Cost-weight trade studies and design-to-cost analysis
responsibilities: Design and analyze aerospace vehicle structures, subsystems, and system architectures to meet mission performance and safety requirements
Conduct structural, thermal, and aerodynamic analyses using FEA and CFD tools to validate design margins across flight envelopes
Develop and maintain system-level requirements using MBSE methodologies and manage requirement traceability in DOORS
Lead multidisciplinary design reviews (PDR, CDR) and coordinate with propulsion, avionics, and manufacturing teams to resolve design conflicts
Perform trade studies and design optimization to balance mass, cost, performance, and schedule constraints for aerospace programs
Author detailed engineering documentation including design specifications, interface control documents (ICDs), and verification plans
Support ground testing, qualification campaigns, and flight readiness reviews by analyzing test data and validating against models
Collaborate with suppliers and subcontractors to ensure aerospace component compliance with design and quality standards (AS9100)
Identify and mitigate technical risks using failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA) and fault tree analysis (FTA)
Develop parametric CAD models and detailed engineering drawings in CATIA or SolidWorks for fabrication and assembly
